idea-plugin.name: TODO Highlighter & Comment Manager
idea-plugin.description:
TODO Highlighter & Comment Manager highlights and tracks TODO, FIXME, HACK, NOTE, and XXX comments across your source code — in any language, with editor highlighting, a dedicated tool window, structured metadata, and two-way editing.

It understands a structured comment format:

// TODO [tag] (priority) description

Features

  • Tool window with a tree view of every TODO in the project, grouped by file, tag, or priority.
  • Locate & preview — show each item's file name or full path, hover for its path in a tooltip, and optionally preview items in a single reusable tab on click.
  • Bidirectional editing — change a TODO's description, tag, or priority from the panel and the source comment is rewritten in place (with full undo support).
  • Editor highlighting — keywords, tags, priorities, description text, and comment delimiters are colorized directly in the editor.
  • Add & complete — insert new TODOs at the caret, or mark them done.
  • Precise matching — choose how keyword case is matched (any / upper-only / lower-only) and optionally require a keyword to be the first word on its line, so words like "note" mid-sentence aren't picked up. Optionally suppress the IDE's own built-in TODO highlighting so it doesn't double up with this plugin.
  • Fully configurable — customize keywords and all colors under Settings > Tools > TODO Manager.
  • Auto-refresh as you edit files.

Works in all IntelliJ-based IDEs — IntelliJ IDEA, WebStorm, PyCharm, GoLand, PhpStorm, Rider, CLion, RubyMine, Android Studio, and more.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.7.0

  • File path in the tool window — each item can show just the file name (default), the path from the project root, or the full absolute path. Configure it under Settings > Tools > TODO Manager.
  • Hover tooltip — hovering an item reveals its path (project-relative by default, or absolute / file name), handy for reading long paths at a glance. It can be turned off entirely.
  • Preview on single click — optionally open items in the editor's reusable preview tab with a single click, so clicking through TODOs doesn't pile up tabs. Editing the file or double-clicking promotes it to a normal tab. (Turning this on enables the IDE's "Open files in preview tab" option; it is restored when turned off.)

1.6.1

  • Fix — the getting-started tour is now offered through a first-run notification instead of opening automatically as a dialog, so it never interrupts startup (and no longer blocks automated IDE/plugin-install checks). Click Start tour in the notification, or open it any time from Help > TODO Manager: Getting Started.

1.6.0

  • Getting-started tour — a short, visual onboarding shown on first run: how markers are highlighted, the KEYWORD [tag] (priority) description format, and where the tool window lives. It can drop you straight into creating your first TODO.
  • Re-open the tour any time from Help > TODO Manager: Getting Started.

1.5.0

  • Scans the whole project by default — TODOs in folders with any name (and their sub-folders) are now picked up, instead of only auto-detected source directories. Excluded and IDE-ignored folders are still skipped, and source-only scanning can be re-enabled in settings.
  • Empty-state guidance — when there is nothing to show, the tool window now says so; if scanning is limited to source directories but none exist, it offers a link straight into the settings to add one.
  • Fix — no longer throws a Read access is allowed from inside read-action only error when marking a TODO as done or deleting it.
  • Fix — the settings page now scrolls with the mouse wheel, not only the scrollbar.

1.4.0

  • Group by keyword — a new Keyword option in the Group by menu.
  • Keyword filter — a toolbar dropdown to show only items with a chosen keyword, or All to show every keyword.

1.3.1

  • Collapse by default — a Collapse toolbar toggle to start groups collapsed instead of fully expanded.
  • Expand/collapse state preserved on refresh — refreshing keeps each group the way you left it; newly appearing groups follow the collapse-by-default setting.
  • Reverse sort — a Reverse toolbar toggle that flips the group order and the items within each group.
  • Live updates — the tool window now refreshes as TODOs are added, changed, or removed in the editor, without waiting for the file to be saved.
  • Fix — no longer logs a write-unsafe context error while typing; the live re-scan now runs only once the edited document's PSI is committed.

1.2.1

  • Fix — no longer throws an IndexOutOfBoundsException when scanning a file whose content has just changed (the comment offsets could briefly outrun the reloaded document). Affected files now re-scan once the editor catches up.

1.2.0

  • Keyword case matching — choose Any case, Upper-case only, or Lower-case only so a lower-case note in prose isn't mistaken for the NOTE keyword.
  • First-word matching — optionally only recognize a keyword when it's the first word on its line, so keywords appearing mid-sentence are ignored.
  • Suppress IDE TODO highlighting — optionally clear the IDE's own built-in TODO highlighting so it doesn't double up with this plugin (restored when turned off).

1.1.0

  • Create general (code-free) TODOs from the panel — check "General TODO" in the New TODO dialog to track items that aren't attached to any comment.
  • Mark as Done now stamps the completion with your git user name and the date, e.g. DONE fix login (done by J4sp3r on 2026-06-29).
  • Completed items stay visible — DONE comments and done general todos are shown struck through with a "done by … on …" label. Toggle them with Show done in the toolbar.

1.0.0

  • Initial release
  • Tool window with tree view of all TODO/FIXME/HACK/NOTE comments
  • Structured comment format: // TODO [tag] (priority) description
  • Group by file, tag, or priority
  • Bidirectional editing: change priority/tag/description in panel → updates source
  • Add new TODOs from the panel
  • Mark TODOs as done
  • Configurable keywords and patterns
  • Auto-refresh on file changes

action.description: Show the TODO Manager onboarding tour
